name: MEZZOTINT
message: Take a picture and transform it into a mezzotint print. Mezzotint is an intaglio printmaking technique where the artist works from dark to light by smoothing a roughened copper plate. The image should feature rich, velvety blacks with luminous highlights emerging from darkness, characteristic of this 17th-century Dutch technique.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Use authentic mezzotint tonal gradations
• Rich velvety black backgrounds
• Smooth tonal transitions from dark to light
• No visible line work - pure tonal technique
• Copper plate printing texture

name: AQUATINT
message: Take a picture and transform it into an aquatint print. Aquatint is an intaglio printmaking process that creates tonal effects by etching areas of a metal plate with acid through a porous ground of resin particles. The result should resemble a watercolor wash rendered in print form, with granular tonal areas and crisp etched lines.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Granular tonal areas characteristic of aquatint
• Watercolor-like wash effects in print form
• Visible plate tone and acid-bitten texture
• Limited color palette typical of printmaking

name: DRYPOINT ETCHING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a drypoint etching. Drypoint is an intaglio printmaking technique where the artist scratches directly into a metal plate with a sharp needle, creating a characteristic soft, velvety burr along the incised lines. The image should have rich, feathery lines with soft edges and limited edition print quality.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Characteristic burr creating soft, fuzzy line edges
• Rich ink accumulation along scratched lines
• Plate tone visible in background
• Hand-pulled print appearance on dampened paper

name: NIHONGA
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Nihonga painting. Nihonga is a traditional Japanese painting style using natural mineral pigments, shell pigments (gofun), and animal-skin glue on washi paper or silk. The image should embody Japanese aesthetic values of subtlety, harmony, and reverence for nature with luminous mineral pigment colors and delicate brushwork.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Use luminous mineral pigment appearance
• Delicate, refined brushwork
• Japanese aesthetic sensibility (wabi-sabi, mono no aware)
• Gold leaf or silver leaf accents where appropriate
• Silk or washi paper texture

name: RINPA SCHOOL
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Rinpa school painting. Rinpa is a 17th-century Japanese decorative art movement founded in Kyoto, characterized by bold compositions on gold leaf backgrounds, vibrant colors, and the tarashikomi technique of pooling wet pigments. The image should feature flat decorative surfaces, stylized natural motifs, and opulent gold backgrounds.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Gold leaf background
• Bold, flat decorative composition
• Tarashikomi wet-on-wet pigment pooling technique
• Vibrant mineral pigment colors
• Stylized natural elements (waves, flowers, clouds)

name: YAMATO-E
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Yamato-e painting. Yamato-e is a classical Japanese painting style from the Heian period (794-1185) characterized by narrative scroll compositions, elevated bird's-eye perspective, blown-off roof technique (fukinuki yatai), and delicate mineral pigment colors on gold-leafed surfaces depicting scenes of Japanese court life and literature.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Elevated bird's-eye perspective
• Fukinuki yatai (blown-off roof) technique
• Delicate mineral pigment colors
• Narrative scroll composition
• Heian period court aesthetic

name: MINHWA KOREAN FOLK PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Korean Minhwa folk painting. Minhwa is traditional Korean folk art produced by itinerant artists, featuring bold colors, symbolic imagery, and decorative compositions depicting tigers, magpies, peonies, and mythical creatures. The style reflects the dreams and aspirations of common Korean people with vibrant, uninhibited artistic expression.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Bold, vibrant colors
• Symbolic Korean folk imagery
• Decorative flat composition
• Traditional Korean paper (hanji) texture
• Uninhibited, expressive brushwork

name: DANCHEONG
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Korean Dancheong decorative painting. Dancheong is the traditional Korean art of ornamental painting applied to wooden buildings, palaces, and temples, dating back to the Goguryeo period (37 BCE-668 CE). The image should feature intricate geometric patterns, lotus flowers, vines, and vibrant traditional colors arranged in symmetrical decorative borders.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Traditional five-color palette (obangsaek)
• Intricate geometric and floral patterns
• Symmetrical decorative borders
• Lotus flower and vine motifs
• Wooden architectural surface texture

name: ADIRE TEXTILE
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Yoruba Adire textile design. Adire is a traditional Nigerian resist-dye technique where Yoruba women create intricate patterns on cloth using indigo dye and various resist methods including starch paste (adire eleko) and tied/stitched fabric (adire oniko). The image should feature deep indigo blue and white patterns with geometric and symbolic Yoruba motifs.

options.text: Adire Eleko style with hand-painted cassava starch resist patterns creating intricate white designs on deep indigo cloth
Adire Oniko style with tied and stitched resist patterns creating organic circular and linear designs in indigo
Adire Alabere style with machine-stitched resist patterns creating fine detailed geometric designs

additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Deep indigo blue and white color palette
• Traditional Yoruba symbolic motifs
• Hand-crafted resist-dye texture
• Cloth/textile surface appearance

name: BOGOLANFINI MUDCLOTH
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Malian Bogolanfini (mudcloth) design. Bogolanfini is a traditional Malian textile art where handwoven cotton cloth is painted with fermented mud to create bold geometric patterns in earth tones. Each symbol carries cultural meaning passed down through generations of Bambara women artists.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Earth-toned color palette (browns, blacks, creams)
• Bold geometric symbolic patterns
• Handwoven cotton cloth texture
• Fermented mud paint appearance
• Traditional Bambara symbolic motifs

name: KENTE CLOTH
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Ghanaian Kente cloth design. Kente is a traditional Ghanaian hand-woven textile featuring vibrant, colorful geometric strip patterns created on narrow looms and sewn together. Each color and pattern combination carries symbolic meaning in Akan culture, traditionally worn during ceremonial occasions.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Vibrant multi-colored geometric strip patterns
• Hand-woven textile texture
• Traditional Akan symbolic color combinations
• Interlocking geometric designs
• Silk and cotton thread appearance

name: SHIBORI
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Japanese Shibori textile design. Shibori is a Japanese manual resist-dyeing technique that creates three-dimensional patterns through folding, crumpling, stitching, plaiting, or twisting fabric before dyeing. The image should feature organic indigo patterns with characteristic white resist areas and dimensional textile texture.

options.text: Kanoko Shibori (bound resist) creating small dotted tie-dye patterns
Arashi Shibori (pole-wrapping) creating diagonal stripe patterns
Itajime Shibori (folded and clamped) creating geometric repeat patterns
Kumo Shibori (spider web) creating radiating circular patterns

additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Indigo blue and white color palette
• Three-dimensional textile texture
• Organic resist-dye patterns
• Hand-crafted imperfections
• Fabric fold and crumple marks visible

name: IKAT WEAVING
message: Take a picture and transform it into an Ikat woven textile design. Ikat is a complex handweaving technique practiced across Southeast Asia, Central Asia, and Latin America where threads are selectively bound and dyed before weaving, creating characteristic blurred-edge patterns. The image should feature the distinctive feathered edges of ikat patterns with rich, saturated dye colors.

options.text: Indonesian double ikat (geringsing) with intricate ceremonial patterns
Uzbek silk ikat (atlas) with bold abstract cloud-like patterns in vibrant colors
Japanese kasuri ikat with indigo geometric patterns on cotton

additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Characteristic blurred/feathered pattern edges
• Rich saturated dye colors
• Woven textile texture
• Pre-dyed thread pattern appearance

name: WAYANG KULIT
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Javanese Wayang Kulit shadow puppet. Wayang Kulit is a traditional Indonesian shadow puppet art form over 1,000 years old, where intricately carved and perforated leather puppets are illuminated from behind to cast detailed shadows. The image should transform the subject into an ornate leather puppet with characteristic Javanese stylization, elaborate headdresses, and intricate perforated patterns.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Intricate leather carving and perforation patterns
• Characteristic Javanese puppet proportions and stylization
• Backlit shadow projection appearance
• Gold leaf and paint details on leather
• Articulated jointed limbs

name: CELADON GLAZE
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Korean celadon ceramic artwork. Celadon is a translucent, pale jade-green glaze achieved through reduction firing of iron oxide in stoneware or porcelain. The image should appear as if carved or inlaid into a celadon-glazed ceramic surface with the characteristic luminous blue-green color, crackle patterns, and sanggam inlay technique of Goryeo dynasty Korean celadon.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Translucent jade-green celadon glaze color
• Characteristic crackle pattern in glaze
• Carved or inlaid (sanggam) design technique
• Smooth ceramic surface texture
• Goryeo dynasty aesthetic

name: RAKU CERAMIC
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Raku ceramic artwork. Raku is a Japanese ceramic technique rooted in Zen philosophy, first produced in 16th-century Kyoto for tea ceremony vessels. The image should feature the characteristic crackle glaze, metallic lusters, carbon-blackened unglazed areas, and the wabi-sabi aesthetic of beautiful imperfection created by rapid firing and reduction cooling.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Crackle glaze with carbon penetration
• Metallic copper and gold luster effects
• Carbon-blackened unglazed areas
• Wabi-sabi aesthetic of imperfection
• Handmade ceramic surface texture

name: MAJOLICA CERAMIC
message: Take a picture and transform it into an Italian Majolica ceramic painting. Majolica is a Renaissance-era decorative ceramic technique where colorful metallic oxide glazes are painted over an opaque tin-based white glaze on earthenware. The image should feature the vibrant painterly decoration, rich cobalt blues, manganese purples, copper greens, and antimony yellows characteristic of Italian Renaissance maiolica pottery.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Opaque white tin-glaze background
• Vibrant metallic oxide colors
• Painterly brushwork on ceramic surface
• Renaissance-era decorative motifs
• Earthenware ceramic texture

name: COLLAGRAPH PRINT
message: Take a picture and transform it into a collagraph print. A collagraph is a printmaking technique where a collage of textured materials (cardboard, fabric, leaves, string, sand) is glued to a plate, sealed, inked, and printed. The image should feature rich textural variety, embossed paper quality, and the unique combination of relief and intaglio printing effects.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Rich variety of textures from collaged materials
• Embossed paper impression
• Combined relief and intaglio ink effects
• Handmade plate irregularities
• Dampened printmaking paper texture

name: CHINE-COLLÉ
message: Take a picture and transform it into a chine-collé print. Chine-collé is a printmaking technique where thin sheets of colored or decorative paper are adhered to a heavier printing paper during the printing process, combining printed imagery with delicate collaged paper elements. The image should feature layered translucent papers with printed imagery creating depth and color variation.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Layered thin papers of different colors and textures
• Printed imagery over collaged paper
• Delicate translucent paper edges visible
• Fine art printmaking quality
• Subtle color variations from paper layers

name: VISCOSITY PRINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a viscosity print. Viscosity printing is an experimental intaglio technique developed by Stanley William Hayter where inks of different viscosities are applied to a single plate, causing them to repel each other and create complex multi-color prints in a single pass. The image should feature rich overlapping colors with organic separation patterns.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Multiple ink colors separating organically
• Rich intaglio plate texture
• Complex color interactions from viscosity differences
• Single-pass multi-color printing appearance
• Experimental fine art printmaking quality

name: CLOISONNÉ ENAMEL
message: Take a picture and transform it into a cloisonné enamel artwork. Cloisonné is an ancient metalworking technique where thin metal wire strips (cloisons) are soldered to a metal surface to create compartments that are filled with colored enamel paste and fired. The image should feature bold outlines of gold or copper wire separating vibrant jewel-toned enamel colors with a smooth, glossy fired surface.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Thin gold or copper wire outlines (cloisons)
• Vibrant jewel-toned enamel colors
• Smooth glossy fired enamel surface
• Metal base visible at edges
• Traditional decorative motifs

name: CHAMPLEVÉ ENAMEL
message: Take a picture and transform it into a champlevé enamel artwork. Champlevé is a medieval metalworking technique where troughs are carved or cast into a thick metal plate, filled with vitreous enamel, fired, and polished flush with the metal surface. The image should feature the characteristic appearance of colored enamel fields separated by raised metal ridges, typical of Romanesque and Gothic decorative arts.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Carved metal troughs filled with enamel
• Raised copper or bronze metal ridges
• Polished flush surface
• Medieval decorative aesthetic
• Limoges enamel color palette

name: PIETRA DURA
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Pietra Dura stone inlay artwork. Pietra Dura (also called commesso) is an Italian decorative art technique where precisely cut pieces of semi-precious stones (lapis lazuli, malachite, jasper, agate) are fitted together like a jigsaw puzzle to create images on marble surfaces. The image should feature the luminous, polished stone surfaces and precise geometric fitting characteristic of Florentine workshops.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Precisely cut semi-precious stone pieces
• Polished stone surface with natural veining
• Rich colors from natural minerals
• Black marble or slate background
• Florentine Renaissance craftsmanship

name: SGRAFITTO POTTERY
message: Take a picture and transform it into a sgraffito pottery design. Sgraffito is a ceramic decoration technique where a layer of colored slip or glaze is applied over a contrasting clay body, then scratched through to reveal the color beneath, creating linear designs. The image should feature bold scratched-through line work revealing contrasting clay colors beneath the surface slip.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Scratched-through surface revealing contrasting color
• Bold linear design work
• Ceramic slip and clay body texture
• Hand-carved line quality
• Traditional pottery surface

name: PERSIAN MINIATURE
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Persian miniature painting. Persian miniature painting is a richly detailed art form from Iran dating to the 13th century, characterized by intricate detail, vibrant colors, gold leaf illumination, flat perspective, and elaborate decorative borders. Subjects are depicted with idealized features within lush garden settings or architectural frames.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Intricate fine detail and miniature scale
• Vibrant gouache and gold leaf colors
• Flat perspective with multiple viewpoints
• Elaborate decorative borders and margins
• Lush garden or architectural settings

name: MUGHAL MINIATURE
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Mughal miniature painting. Mughal miniature painting flourished in the Indian subcontinent from the 16th to 19th centuries under Mughal patronage, blending Persian, Indian, and European influences. The image should feature meticulous naturalistic detail, rich jewel-toned colors, gold embellishment, and the characteristic Mughal profile portrait style with elaborate costume and architectural settings.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Meticulous naturalistic detail
• Rich jewel-toned pigments and gold
• Characteristic profile or three-quarter view
• Elaborate Mughal costume and jewelry
• Architectural or garden settings with fine detail

name: RAJPUT PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Rajput painting. Rajput painting (also called Rajasthani painting) developed in the royal courts of Rajputana in India from the 16th to 19th centuries. The style features bold colors, strong outlines, flat areas of color, expressive faces, and themes from Hindu epics and romantic poetry, with distinctive regional schools including Mewar, Bundi, and Kishangarh.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Bold flat areas of vibrant color
• Strong dark outlines
• Expressive stylized faces
• Indian miniature painting scale
• Decorative borders and text panels

name: TANJORE PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Tanjore painting. Tanjore (Thanjavur) painting is a classical South Indian art form from Tamil Nadu dating to the 16th century, characterized by rich gold leaf overlay, semi-precious stone embellishments, vibrant colors, and three-dimensional surface decoration depicting Hindu deities and saints on wooden panels.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Rich gold leaf overlay and embossing
• Semi-precious stone and glass bead embellishments
• Vibrant colors on wooden panel
• Three-dimensional raised surface decoration
• Ornate arch frame and decorative border

name: PICHWAI PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Pichwai painting. Pichwai is a traditional Indian painting style from Nathdwara, Rajasthan, created as devotional cloth hangings for temples. The style features intricate detail, rich dark backgrounds, lotus motifs, cows, and elaborate floral patterns painted on cloth with natural pigments and gold detailing.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Rich dark background (typically black or deep blue)
• Intricate lotus and floral motifs
• Natural pigment colors with gold detailing
• Cloth painting texture
• Devotional art aesthetic

name: KALIGHAT PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Kalighat painting. Kalighat painting originated in 19th-century Calcutta near the Kalighat temple, featuring bold sweeping brushstrokes, minimal backgrounds, strong outlines, and flat areas of color. Originally depicting Hindu deities, the style evolved into social and political commentary with characteristic simplified forms and dynamic compositions.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Bold sweeping brushstrokes
• Minimal or plain backgrounds
• Strong dark outlines
• Flat areas of vibrant color
• Simplified dynamic forms

name: ETHIOPIAN ICONOGRAPHY
message: Take a picture and transform it into an Ethiopian Orthodox Christian icon painting. Ethiopian iconography is a distinctive African Christian art tradition dating to the 4th century, characterized by large almond-shaped eyes, frontal poses for holy figures, profile views for evil characters, bold outlines, flat vibrant colors, and geometric patterned backgrounds on wooden panels or parchment.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Large almond-shaped eyes with direct gaze
• Frontal pose with stylized proportions
• Bold dark outlines
• Flat vibrant colors (especially reds, blues, yellows)
• Geometric patterned backgrounds

name: NDEBELE HOUSE PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a South African Ndebele house painting. Ndebele house painting is a vibrant geometric mural art tradition of the Ndebele people of South Africa, featuring bold black outlines filled with bright primary and secondary colors in symmetrical geometric patterns painted on exterior walls as expressions of cultural identity.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Bold black outlines
• Bright primary and secondary colors
• Symmetrical geometric patterns
• Wall/architectural surface texture
• Traditional Ndebele motifs (chevrons, triangles, rectangles)

name: TAPA CLOTH
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Pacific Island tapa cloth design. Tapa cloth (also called bark cloth) is a traditional Polynesian and Melanesian art form made from beaten inner bark of the paper mulberry tree, decorated with geometric patterns using natural pigments. The image should feature bold geometric designs in earth tones on a fibrous bark cloth surface.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Beaten bark cloth texture
• Bold geometric patterns
• Earth-toned natural pigments (brown, black, rust)
• Traditional Polynesian motifs
• Hand-stamped or painted appearance

name: ABORIGINAL BARK PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into an Australian Aboriginal bark painting. Aboriginal bark painting is a traditional art form from Arnhem Land in northern Australia, painted on flattened eucalyptus bark using natural ochre pigments. The style features cross-hatching (rarrk), X-ray style depicting internal anatomy, and symbolic representations of Dreamtime stories.

options.text: X-ray style showing internal skeletal and organ structures of the subject in traditional ochre colors
Cross-hatching (rarrk) style with fine parallel lines creating tonal variation in ochre pigments
Figurative Dreamtime narrative style with symbolic representations and journey lines

additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Natural ochre pigments (red, yellow, white, black)
• Eucalyptus bark surface texture
• Traditional cross-hatching patterns
• Symbolic Aboriginal motifs
• Flat composition without Western perspective

name: INUIT PRINTMAKING
message: Take a picture and transform it into an Inuit print in the style of Cape Dorset printmaking. Inuit printmaking from the Canadian Arctic features bold simplified forms, limited color palettes, and subjects drawn from Arctic life, mythology, and nature. The style uses stonecut, stencil, and lithographic techniques with characteristic flat areas of color and strong graphic compositions.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Bold simplified graphic forms
• Limited color palette
• Flat areas of solid color
• Strong graphic composition
• Stonecut or stencil print texture

name: HAIDA FORMLINE
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Haida formline art design. Formline art is the traditional art style of the Haida, Tlingit, and other Pacific Northwest Coast Indigenous peoples, characterized by flowing ovoid and U-form shapes, bold black primary formlines, red secondary elements, and blue-green tertiary fills depicting stylized animals and supernatural beings.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Flowing ovoid and U-form shapes
• Bold black primary formlines
• Red secondary design elements
• Blue-green tertiary fills
• Traditional Northwest Coast stylization

name: PUEBLO POTTERY
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Pueblo pottery design. Pueblo pottery is a traditional Native American ceramic art from the American Southwest, featuring hand-coiled vessels decorated with geometric and naturalistic designs using mineral and plant-based pigments. Each pueblo has distinctive styles, from Acoma's fine-line geometric patterns to San Ildefonso's black-on-black polished ware.

options.text: Acoma Pueblo style with fine-line geometric patterns in black and white on thin-walled pottery
San Ildefonso black-on-black style with matte designs on highly polished blackware
Hopi pottery style with bold geometric and bird motifs in earth tones on yellow-orange clay

additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Hand-coiled pottery surface
• Traditional mineral and plant pigments
• Geometric and naturalistic motifs
• Pueblo-specific design traditions
• Polished or matte clay surface

name: PERSIAN CARPET DESIGN
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Persian carpet design. Persian carpet weaving is an ancient Iranian art form featuring intricate hand-knotted patterns with central medallions, elaborate borders, and dense floral or geometric motifs. The image should appear as a finely knotted wool or silk carpet with rich jewel-toned colors and the characteristic symmetrical composition of Isfahan, Tabriz, or Kashan traditions.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Intricate hand-knotted texture
• Rich jewel-toned color palette
• Central medallion composition
• Elaborate multi-layered borders
• Dense floral arabesque patterns

name: IZNIK TILE
message: Take a picture and transform it into an Iznik ceramic tile design. Iznik pottery is a decorated Ottoman-era ceramic art from the Turkish city of Iznik (ancient Nicaea), famous for its cobalt blue, turquoise, emerald green, and distinctive raised coral red (Armenian bole) colors depicting tulips, carnations, hyacinths, and arabesque patterns on a brilliant white ground.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Brilliant white ceramic ground
• Cobalt blue, turquoise, green, and coral red palette
• Tulip, carnation, and arabesque motifs
• Raised coral red (Armenian bole) details
• Ottoman decorative aesthetic

name: ZELLIGE MOSAIC
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Moroccan Zellige mosaic tile design. Zellige is a traditional Moroccan art of hand-cut geometric mosaic tilework, where individually chiseled glazed terracotta pieces are assembled into complex mathematical star and polygon patterns. The image should feature intricate interlocking geometric patterns in traditional Moroccan colors.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Hand-cut geometric tile pieces
• Complex interlocking star and polygon patterns
• Traditional Moroccan color palette (blue, green, white, yellow)
• Glazed terracotta tile texture
• Mathematical precision in pattern geometry

name: DELFT BLUE
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Dutch Delft Blue ceramic tile or plate design. Delftware is a traditional Dutch tin-glazed earthenware featuring hand-painted cobalt blue designs on white backgrounds, originally inspired by Chinese porcelain. The image should feature the characteristic blue-and-white palette with painterly brushwork depicting landscapes, figures, or floral motifs within decorative borders.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Cobalt blue on white tin-glaze palette
• Hand-painted brushwork quality
• Decorative corner motifs and borders
• Ceramic tile or plate format
• 17th-century Dutch aesthetic

name: WILLOW PATTERN
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Willow Pattern ceramic design. The Willow Pattern is an iconic blue-and-white transferware design created in 18th-century England, depicting a Chinese-inspired landscape with a pagoda, willow tree, bridge with figures, boat, and pair of doves. The image should transform the subject into this distinctive narrative ceramic illustration style.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Blue-and-white transferware palette
• Chinese-inspired landscape elements
• Engraved line quality of transfer printing
• Decorative geometric border
• Ceramic plate or dish format

name: UKIYO-E WOODBLOCK
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Japanese Ukiyo-e woodblock print. Ukiyo-e (pictures of the floating world) flourished from the 17th to 19th centuries, featuring bold outlines, flat areas of color, and distinctive compositions depicting kabuki actors, beautiful women, landscapes, and nature. The image should feature the characteristic woodblock printing texture with visible wood grain, registration marks, and water-based pigment colors.

options.text: Hokusai style with dynamic natural forms, bold compositions, and Prussian blue palette
Hiroshige style with atmospheric landscapes, rain effects, and seasonal poetry
Utamaro style with elegant portraits, subtle color gradients, and mica backgrounds
Sharaku style with dramatic kabuki actor portraits and exaggerated expressions

additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Bold outlines with flat color areas
• Woodblock printing texture and wood grain
• Water-based pigment colors
• Traditional Japanese composition
• Bokashi gradient printing technique

name: SOSAKU HANGA
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Sosaku Hanga (creative print). Sosaku Hanga is a 20th-century Japanese printmaking movement where the artist personally designs, carves, and prints the work (as opposed to the collaborative ukiyo-e system). The style emphasizes individual artistic expression, bold graphic compositions, and experimental techniques while maintaining Japanese aesthetic sensibility.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Bold graphic composition
• Individual artistic expression
• Woodblock or linocut printing texture
• Modern Japanese aesthetic
• Experimental color and form

name: SHIN HANGA
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Shin Hanga (new print) woodblock print. Shin Hanga was an early 20th-century Japanese art movement that revitalized traditional ukiyo-e woodblock printing with Western elements like light effects and individualism. The style features soft atmospheric effects, subtle color gradations, and romantic depictions of landscapes, women, and actors.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Soft atmospheric light effects
• Subtle color gradations (bokashi)
• Romantic, idealized aesthetic
• Fine woodblock printing quality
• Blend of Japanese and Western sensibilities

name: GONGBI PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Chinese Gongbi (meticulous) painting. Gongbi is a traditional Chinese painting technique characterized by extremely fine, detailed brushwork using ink and mineral pigments on silk or sized paper. Unlike the spontaneous xieyi (freehand) style, Gongbi requires careful planning, precise outlines, and multiple layers of translucent color washes to build up rich, luminous imagery.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Extremely fine detailed brushwork
• Precise ink outlines
• Multiple translucent color wash layers

name: CUBO-FUTURISM
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Russian Cubo-Futurist painting. Cubo-Futurism (1912-1915) was a Russian avant-garde movement combining French Cubist fragmentation with Italian Futurist dynamism and speed. The style features angular geometric forms, dynamic diagonal compositions, metallic colors, and the energy of modern industrial life.

name: SYNCHROMISM
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Synchromist painting. Synchromism (1912-1920s) was an American abstract art movement founded by Morgan Russell and Stanton Macdonald-Wright, based on the idea that color and music are analogous phenomena. The style features rhythmic arrangements of pure spectral colors in overlapping curved forms creating visual harmonies.

name: CAMDEN TOWN STYLE
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Camden Town Group painting. The Camden Town Group (1911-1913) was a British Post-Impressionist movement depicting everyday urban life in London with bold color, thick paint application, and intimate domestic scenes. The style features muted but rich colors, strong tonal contrasts, and the unglamorous reality of working-class interiors.

name: NEUE WILDE
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Neue Wilde (New Wild Ones) painting. Neue Wilde was a German Neo-Expressionist movement of the early 1980s featuring aggressive, spontaneous painting with raw emotional energy, bold colors, crude figuration, and deliberate rejection of conceptual art's intellectualism in favor of visceral painterly expression.

name: TRANSAVANGUARDIA
message: Take a picture and transform it into an Italian Transavanguardia painting. Transavanguardia (1979-1980s) was an Italian Neo-Expressionist movement championed by critic Achille Bonito Oliva, featuring a return to figurative painting with mythological references, Mediterranean colors, and a nomadic wandering between historical styles and personal mythology.

name: COBRA PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a CoBrA movement painting. CoBrA (Copenhagen, Brussels, Amsterdam, 1948-1951) was a European avant-garde movement featuring spontaneous, childlike expression with bright colors, thick impasto paint, primitive figuration, and the rejection of Western cultural tradition in favor of raw creative energy inspired by children's art and folk art.

name: LETTRISM
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Lettrist artwork. Lettrism (Lettrisme), founded by Isidore Isou in Paris in 1945, is an avant-garde movement that reduces art to its basic elements - letters, signs, and symbols. The image should feature the subject constructed from or overlaid with typographic characters, alphabetic fragments, and symbolic notation.

name: SITUATIONIST DÉTOURNEMENT
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Situationist détournement artwork. Détournement is a technique developed by the Situationist International (1957-1972) of subversively repurposing existing cultural materials - comics, advertisements, films - by altering their meaning through juxtaposition, altered speech bubbles, and critical recontextualization.

name: FLUXUS EVENT SCORE
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Fluxus event score or instruction piece. Fluxus (1960s-70s) was an international avant-garde network creating simple, playful instruction-based artworks. The image should transform the subject into a minimalist typographic instruction card or diagram in the style of Yoko Ono's instruction paintings or George Brecht's event scores.

name: MAIL ART
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Mail Art (Correspondence Art) piece. Mail Art is a worldwide artistic movement using the postal system as a medium, featuring decorated envelopes, artist stamps (artistamps), rubber stamp art, collaged postcards, and mixed-media postal objects. The image should feature postal elements, stamps, cancellation marks, and collaged artistic decoration.

name: CONCRETE POETRY
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Concrete Poetry (visual poetry) artwork. Concrete poetry is a literary-visual art form where the typographic arrangement of words on the page creates a visual image that reinforces the poem's meaning. The image should transform the subject into a shape made entirely of carefully arranged text, where the words form the visual outline and texture of the subject.

name: PHOTOREALIST WATERCOLOR
message: Take a picture and transform it into a photorealist watercolor painting. Photorealist watercolor combines the transparent luminosity of watercolor medium with extreme photographic detail and precision. The image should feature the characteristic wet-on-wet blooms, hard and soft edges, transparent color glazing, and white paper highlights of watercolor while maintaining photographic accuracy.

name: PLEIN AIR PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a plein air painting. Plein air (open air) painting is the practice of painting outdoors to capture natural light, atmosphere, and color directly from observation. The image should feature the characteristic spontaneous brushwork, vibrant natural light effects, visible paint texture, and the fresh immediacy of painting completed in a single outdoor session.

name: TONALIST LANDSCAPE
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Tonalist landscape painting. Tonalism (1880s-1920s) was an American art movement featuring soft, muted, atmospheric landscapes dominated by a single overall tone. The style emphasizes mood over detail, with misty veils of color, indistinct forms, and the poetic suggestion of twilight, dawn, or fog.

name: CLOISONNISM PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Cloisonnist painting. Cloisonnism (1880s-1890s) was a Post-Impressionist style developed by Émile Bernard and Louis Anquetin, featuring bold flat areas of color separated by dark outlines, inspired by cloisonné enamelwork and stained glass. The style influenced Gauguin's Synthetist paintings.

STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

name: PONT-AVEN SCHOOL
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Pont-Aven School painting. The Pont-Aven School was a group of artists working in Pont-Aven, Brittany in the 1880s-90s, led by Paul Gauguin. The style (also called Synthetism) features simplified forms, flat areas of bold color, strong outlines, and the synthesis of observed reality with memory, emotion, and imagination.

name: INTIMISM
message: Take a picture and transform it into an Intimist painting. Intimism was a Post-Impressionist style associated with Pierre Bonnard and Édouard Vuillard, depicting quiet domestic interiors with rich decorative patterns, warm ambient light, and the intimate poetry of everyday private life. The style features complex patterned surfaces and figures absorbed in daily activities.

name: MACCHIAIOLI
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Macchiaioli painting. The Macchiaioli were a group of Italian painters active in Tuscany in the 1850s-70s who painted with bold patches (macchie) of color and strong chiaroscuro, predating French Impressionism. The style features bold tonal contrasts, patches of flat color, outdoor light, and scenes of Italian daily life and landscape.

name: LUMINISM PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Luminist painting. Luminism was a 19th-century American landscape painting style characterized by attention to the effects of light, smooth invisible brushwork, calm atmospheric conditions, and a sense of quiet contemplation. The style features mirror-like water reflections, hazy atmospheric light, and meticulous hidden brushstrokes.

name: TENEBRISM PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Tenebrist painting. Tenebrism is a dramatic Baroque painting technique using extreme contrasts between deep darkness and intense illumination, associated with Caravaggio and his followers. The image should feature a single dramatic light source illuminating the subject against an almost completely black background.

name: VEDUTA PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Veduta (view painting). Veduta is an 18th-century Italian genre of highly detailed, large-scale topographic paintings of cityscapes, particularly Venice, Rome, and other grand European cities. Associated with Canaletto and Bellotto, the style features precise architectural detail, atmospheric perspective, and documentary accuracy.

name: CAPRICCIO PAINTING

The image should place the subject within a fantastical architectural landscape combining classical ruins, grand buildings, and imaginary structures.

Quadratura is a Baroque illusionistic painting technique creating the appearance of three-dimensional architecture on flat ceilings and walls through extreme foreshortening and perspective. The image should feature dramatic di sotto in sù (from below upward) perspective with painted architectural elements opening to sky.

Anamorphosis is a distorted projection technique where an image appears normal only when viewed from a specific angle or reflected in a curved mirror. The image should feature the characteristic extreme elongation or distortion that resolves into a recognizable image from the correct viewpoint, as in Holbein's 'The Ambassadors' skull.

The image should feature the subject rendered as objects pinned to a board, tucked behind ribbons, or arranged on a shelf with extreme photographic realism in oil paint.

The image should incorporate the subject alongside traditional vanitas symbols: skulls, extinguished candles, hourglasses, wilting flowers, and decaying fruit.

Pronkstilleven is a lavish subgenre of Dutch Golden Age still life featuring extravagant displays of luxury objects: silver vessels, Venetian glass, Chinese porcelain, lobsters, exotic fruits, and rich fabrics cascading from tables. Singerie is a decorative art genre popular in Rococo France depicting monkeys (singes) engaged in human activities - painting, playing music, dining, or dressing in fashionable clothing. Fête Galante is a Rococo painting genre invented by Antoine Watteau depicting elegant figures in outdoor pastoral settings engaged in courtship, music, and leisure. Chinoiserie is the European artistic interpretation of Chinese and East Asian aesthetics popular in the 17th-18th centuries, featuring fanciful pagodas, exotic birds, flowering branches, and figures in imagined Chinese costume. Turquerie was the 18th-century European fashion for Turkish and Ottoman-inspired art and design, depicting subjects in turbans, flowing robes, and exotic settings with rich textiles, hookahs, and imagined harem scenes. Orientalism was a Western art movement depicting romanticized scenes of North Africa and the Middle East with rich colors, dramatic light, and exotic settings. The style features detailed architectural interiors, bazaar scenes, desert landscapes, and figures in traditional dress rendered with academic painting technique.

The Troubadour Style was a 19th-century French painting movement depicting romanticized medieval and Renaissance historical scenes with meticulous period detail, rich colors, and anecdotal narrative. The Nazarenes were a group of early 19th-century German Romantic painters who sought to revive the spirituality and craftsmanship of medieval and early Renaissance art. The style features clear outlines, pure colors, simple compositions, and the devotional sincerity of early Italian and German masters.

Biedermeier (1815-1848) was a Central European artistic period emphasizing domestic comfort, bourgeois values, and intimate realism. The style features detailed interior scenes, family portraits, flower paintings, and landscapes rendered with precise craftsmanship, warm lighting, and a sense of cozy domesticity.

name: AMERICAN SCENE PAINTING
message: Take a picture and transform it into an American Scene painting. American Scene painting (1920s-1950s) depicted everyday American life, landscapes, and small-town scenes in a realistic, accessible style. The movement includes both Regionalists (Grant Wood, Thomas Hart Benton) and Social Realists, featuring clear compositions, strong forms, and distinctly American subject matter.

Magic Realism in visual art (distinct from literary magic realism) is a painting style featuring precisely rendered realistic imagery with an uncanny, mysterious, or dreamlike quality. Associated with artists like Andrew Wyeth and George Tooker, the style creates unease through hyper-detailed realism combined with strange stillness and ambiguous narrative.

Verism, practiced by Otto Dix and George Grosz in 1920s Weimar Germany, featured grotesque satirical social commentary, unflinching depictions of war veterans, prostitutes, and corrupt bourgeoisie with sharp-focus detail and biting social criticism.

George Grosz style with grotesque satirical caricatures of Weimar society

Used by photographers like Alfred Stieglitz and Edward Steichen, the process creates prints with the depth and richness of fine art etchings.

The style features diffused light, soft edges, painterly tonality, and subjects treated with the same artistic sensibility as painting.

The style features extreme clarity, geometric forms, and the pure photographic medium.

New Topographics (1975) was a photography movement documenting the altered American landscape - suburban sprawl, industrial parks, tract housing, and the intersection of nature and human development. The style features neutral, deadpan observation, frontal compositions, and the banal beauty of man-altered environments.

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Refined hand-painted decoration
• Pristine white porcelain ground
• Gilt gold borders and accents
• European Baroque/Rococo elegance
• Meissen crossed swords quality

name: WEDGWOOD JASPERWARE
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Wedgwood Jasperware design. Jasperware is Wedgwood's iconic unglazed stoneware featuring white bas-relief classical figures and motifs applied to a colored (typically pale blue, sage green, or black) matte background. The style depicts neoclassical scenes from Greek and Roman mythology with elegant white relief figures.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

name: ARTS AND CRAFTS TILE
message: Take a picture and transform it into an Arts and Crafts movement ceramic tile design. Arts and Crafts tiles (1880s-1920s) by makers like William De Morgan and Minton featured hand-painted or tube-lined designs of stylized flora, fauna, ships, and mythical creatures in rich glazes. The style features bold outlines, flat decorative patterns, and the handcrafted quality championed by the movement.

name: ENCAUSTIC TILE
message: Take a picture and transform it into a medieval encaustic floor tile design. Encaustic tiles are ceramic tiles where the pattern is created by inlaying different colored clays into the tile body, creating a design that is integral to the tile rather than painted on. The style features geometric and heraldic patterns in earth tones (buff, red, brown, black) characteristic of medieval English churches and abbeys.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Inlaid clay pattern technique
• Earth-toned color palette
• Geometric and heraldic motifs
• Medieval tile format
• Church and abbey floor aesthetic

name: AZULEJO
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Portuguese Azulejo tile panel. Azulejos are traditional Portuguese painted tin-glazed ceramic tiles used to cover building facades and interiors, featuring elaborate narrative scenes, geometric patterns, or figurative compositions predominantly in blue and white. The image should feature the subject rendered across a panel of individual tiles with characteristic blue painting on white glaze.

name: CUERDA SECA
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Cuerda Seca (dry cord) tile design. Cuerda Seca is a ceramic decoration technique from Islamic Spain where a greasy manganese outline prevents different colored glazes from running together during firing, creating crisp multi-colored designs. The style features bold geometric and arabesque patterns with distinct color separations.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Preserve recognizable subject identity
• Dark manganese outline separations
• Multiple distinct glaze colors
• Geometric and arabesque patterns
• Islamic Spanish decorative motifs
• Crisp color boundaries

name: OPUS ANGLICANUM
message: Take a picture and transform it into an Opus Anglicanum embroidery. Opus Anglicanum (English Work) was the finest medieval embroidery in Europe (13th-14th century), created for ecclesiastical vestments and royal garments. The style features intricate gold thread couching, split stitch figures, architectural framing, and religious narrative scenes on silk and velvet grounds.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Gold thread couching technique
• Split stitch figurative work
• Architectural Gothic framing

• Medieval English ecclesiastical aesthetic

name: BARGELLO NEEDLEWORK
message: Take a picture and transform it into a Bargello (Florentine stitch) needlework design. Bargello is a type of needlepoint embroidery featuring dramatic zigzag, flame, or wave patterns created by straight vertical stitches of varying lengths in graduated color sequences. The style creates striking optical effects through rhythmic color progressions.
additionalInstructions:
STYLE ENFORCEMENT:

• Zigzag flame or wave patterns
• Straight vertical stitches
• Graduated color sequences
